question_text: On 21 March, the Sun is directly above the equator. A student in the UK measures the angle of the Sun above the horizon at solar noon. If the angle is 39°, and the UK is at 52°N latitude, explain why this angle is less than 90°.
correct_answer: The Sun is directly above the equator (0° latitude) on the equinox. The UK is at 52°N, so the Sun appears lower in the sky. The angle of elevation equals 90° minus the latitude difference (90° - 52° = 38°, approximately 39° accounting for measurement). The Sun cannot be at 90° above the horizon in the UK because the latitude is too far north.
explanation: During the equinox, the Sun is above the equator. From the UK at 52°N, you're tilted away from the Sun, so it appears lower in the sky. The further north you are, the lower the Sun appears at noon.
